headline: Eos Energy Selected to Deliver Mission-Ready Power for "Golden Dome for America" (U.S. Department of War)
summary: The U.S. Department of War selected Eos under the Golden Dome for America missile-defense initiative to deploy a Z3 zinc-based long-duration storage prototype at a critical defense installation. Leans on Z3's ~91% domestic content, Section 842 NDAA + FEOC compliance and non-flammable safety. A strategically important new defense demand vector — but a prototype selection of undisclosed (multi-million-dollar) size, not yet a large booked order.

headline: Eos pre-announces preliminary Q2: record revenue $68–69M, record ~$807M backlog, margin loss −69/−73%
summary: Preliminary, unaudited Q2 (ended Jun 30): record revenue of $68–69M on a >3× YoY jump in shipments; RECORD backlog ~$807M (+25% QoQ); gross-margin loss narrowing to −69% to −73% (from −78% in Q1); total cash incl. restricted ~$364M (collections ~$78M exceeded revenue). First quarter with both lines commercial — Line 2 ahead of Line 1 on yield/cycle time. Full audited Q2 + call moved to Aug 5.

headline: Frontier Power USA Converts 920 MWh Battery Storage Project Portfolio Under Stella Strategic Framework
summary: FPUSA converted a 920 MWh portfolio (Blanquilla, Aransas Pass, Nash, Wallis) under its Stella Energy Solutions framework — further reservation-to-pipeline conversion in ERCOT. Total FPUSA pipeline cited at ~16 GWh (~2.7 GWh high-probability); closing contingent on the rights-offering funding.

headline: Ofgem LDES Window 1 minded-to decisions — only 16 of 73 projects provisionally awarded; Frontier UK largely excluded
summary: Provisional cap & floor awards skew to pumped hydro + long-duration; of Frontier UK's ~16 projects (~2.6 GW, all 8-hour) only one — Frontier Legacy, 65 MW — made the provisional list. The 'VFB/Zn' category does not confirm Eos vs Invinity tech. Consultation to Aug 7; final awards Autumn 2026.
